items.text: CLIENT-CONTRACT.md defines the seven operations (connect, start, signal, query, cancel, list, describe, subscribe) language-neutrally, each with inputs, outputs, and the errors it can raise.
The contract specifies start idempotency: a retried identical start (same idempotency key + request) returns the original handle; a conflicting reuse raises AlreadyExists.
The contract specifies run targeting: signal/query/cancel/describe target the latest run by default and a specific RunId when given one.
The contract specifies query as a synchronous deadline-bounded round-trip yielding QueryTimeout when the deadline elapses and QueryFailed when the handler errors.

The contract specifies cancellation as a recorded request (cooperative stop), not an immediate kill, and subscribe resumption from the last delivered per-event sequence number with no gaps or duplicates.
The contract defines the shared error taxonomy: NotFound, AlreadyExists, QueryFailed, QueryTimeout, Cancelled, Unavailable, Unauthenticated, InvalidArgument, Server.

ClientBuilder connects to an aion-server endpoint with auth credential and TLS configuration and produces a reusable Client; the Client depends on aion-proto and aion-core, not on aion/aion-store/beamr by default.
Client exposes start, signal, query, cancel, list, and describe over the network transport, returning idiomatic Result<_, ClientError>.
start returns a WorkflowHandle bundling WorkflowId + RunId and offering signal/query/cancel/describe/subscribe as methods; a bare-ID constructor for WorkflowHandle also exists.
Typed payload helpers serialise serde::Serialize inputs to a JSON Payload and deserialise results to serde::DeserializeOwned, with a raw-Payload escape hatch on every payload-bearing operation.
subscribe returns a Stream of decoded events that resumes from the last delivered sequence number across transient disconnects and yields an Err item on terminal failure rather than ending silently.

ClientError is a closed enum covering NotFound, AlreadyExists, QueryFailed, QueryTimeout, Cancelled, Unavailable, Unauthenticated, InvalidArgument, and Server, implementing std::error::Error via thiserror.
An embedded constructor binds the same Client/WorkflowHandle surface to an in-process aion engine behind a feature flag, pulling aion only when that feature is enabled.
Client connects to an aion-server endpoint with auth + TLS config and exposes start, signal, query, cancel, list, and describe; start returns a WorkflowHandle with per-workflow methods.
Payload helpers accept any JSON-serialisable value (and optionally a model / target type for results) with a raw bytes escape hatch on every payload-bearing operation.
subscribe yields an async iterator of decoded events that resumes from the last delivered sequence number across transient disconnects and raises on terminal failure rather than ending silently.

Errors form an exception hierarchy mapping the shared taxonomy (NotFound, AlreadyExists, QueryFailed, QueryTimeout, Cancelled, Unavailable, Unauthenticated, InvalidArgument, Server) with a common base; mypy --strict passes.
Client connects to an aion-server endpoint with auth + TLS config and exposes start, signal, query, cancel, list, and describe; start returns a WorkflowHandle with per-workflow methods.
Payload helpers are generic over input/result types with JSON serialisation and a raw-Payload escape hatch on every payload-bearing operation; the public API is typed while the wire stays Payload.

subscribe returns an AsyncIterable of decoded events that resumes from the last delivered sequence number across transient disconnects and rejects/throws on terminal failure rather than ending silently.
Errors are a discriminated union / class hierarchy mapping the shared taxonomy; tsc --noEmit (strict) and eslint pass.

aion_client connects to an aion-server endpoint with auth + TLS config and exposes connect, start, signal, query, cancel, list, and describe; start returns a workflow handle value usable in per-workflow operations.
Payload helpers take a gleam/json encoder for input and a gleam/dynamic decoder for the result, staying statically typed, with a raw-Payload escape hatch on every payload-bearing operation.
subscribe returns an event stream that resumes from the last delivered sequence number across transient disconnects and surfaces terminal failure through the stream rather than ending silently.
Errors are a Gleam custom type mapping the shared taxonomy; gleam check, gleam format --check, and gleam test pass.

A shared conformance scenario set encodes the start -> signal -> query -> list -> describe -> cancel -> subscribe happy path plus the idempotency-conflict, query-timeout, not-found, and disconnect-resume cases.
Each SDK has a conformance harness that runs the shared scenarios against a real aion-server fixture and asserts the observable results match the contract.
The four SDKs produce identical observable behaviour for every shared scenario (same effect, same returned values, same error variant).
aion-client (crates.io), aion-client-python (PyPI), aion-client-typescript (npm), and aion_client (Hex) each carry complete package metadata, a README, and a type-checked public surface.
Each package ships a runnable example exercising all seven operations against a server, referenced from its README.
